task_completion.description: Draw the 11-sided closed 2D profile of the stair stringer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace canvas shows a closed 2D sketch consisting of 4 vertical lines (risers), 4 horizontal lines (treads), 1 vertical top face, 1 horizontal bottom face, and 1 angled diagonal line connecting the back.

task_completion.description: Apply dimension and equal length constraints to the sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The canvas or property browser displays dimension constraints (e.g., 180 for a riser, 280 for a tread, 200 for the top/bottom faces) and indicates 'Equal Length' constraints applied to the remaining risers and treads.

task_completion.description: Extrude the 2D sketch into a 3D solid with a thickness of 40 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The model is shown in a 3D view as a solid object (not just flat lines), and the property browser or extrusion dialog shows an extrusion depth/thickness value of 40.

task_completion.description: Save the SolveSpace project file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar, a 'Save As' dialog, or a file manager window shows the file 'stair_stringer.slvs' located in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D model as an STL mesh to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog, file manager, or terminal output confirms the creation of 'stair_stringer.stl' in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the SolveSpace interface (toolbars, canvas, property browser) to draw and constrain the model, rather than writing a script or text file to generate it.

integrity.description: The agent used actual parametric constraints rather than freehand drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Constraint symbols (magenta dimensions, equal length markers, horizontal/vertical constraints) are visibly active in the SolveSpace UI, proving the model is parametrically driven.

integrity.description: The agent exported the STL file using the application's built-in functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'File -> Export' menu or a SolveSpace export dialog is visible in the screenshots, showing genuine interaction to generate the STL.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected bounding box dimensions of the final 3D model are 40mm (Z/thickness), 720mm (Y/height: 4 risers * 180mm), and 1120mm (X/width: 4 treads * 280mm). The sketch must have exactly 11 lines and use 'Equal Length' constraints to minimize distance constraints (maximum of 6 distance constraints allowed). The final files must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/stair_stringer.slvs and exported as /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/stair_stringer.stl.
